data.title: Musk's xAI to invest over $20 billion in Mississippi data center
data.description: Jan 9 : ‚ÄåElon Musk's artificial intelligence startup xAI is investing more than $20 billion to build a data center in Southaven, Mississippi, state Governor Tate Reeves said in a statement on Thursday, as a boom in generative AI fuels demand for increased computing power. Interest in data centers swelled ‚Äålast year as tech giants and AI ‚Äåhyperscalers planned and invested billions of dollars in spending to scale up infrastructure. xAI's latest expansion underscores its ambitious push to compete more effectively with industry leaders OpenAI's ChatGPT and Anthropic's Claude by training increasingly advanced models. xAI expects to begin data ‚Äçcenter operations in Southaven in February 2026, the statement added. Musk had announced the purchase of the data center, called "MACROHARDRR", on December 30. The billionaire had said that it would take xAI's compute ‚Äãpower to 2GW, but ‚Äådid not provide investment details or its location at the time.¬Ý¬Ý The data center is close to xAI‚Äôs ‚Äãnewly acquired power plant site in Southaven and its existing data ‚ÅÝcenter in Memphis, Tennessee, according ‚Äåto the governor's statement. Memphis houses xAI's supercomputer cluster known ‚Äãas Colossus, touted as the largest in the world.¬Ý The company has spent $7.8 billion in cash in the first ‚Äçnine months of the year, Bloomberg reported earlier in the day, ‚ÅÝas startups in the AI space typically burn through huge amounts ‚Äãof cash, with billions ‚Äåpoured into acquiring pricey, advanced data center ‚Äçhardware.¬Ý (Reporting ‚Äãby Anusha Shah in Bengaluru)

data.title: Stocks, dollar higher in wake of US jobs report
data.description: NEW YORK/LONDON, Jan 9 : Stock indexes gained and the dollar was higher¬Ýon Friday after data showed the U.S. economy created fewer jobs than expected in December, supporting the view the Federal Reserve would leave interest rates unchanged this month. The Bureau of Labor Statistics monthly report showed 50,000 workers were added to nonfarm payrolls in December, compared with expectations in a Reuters poll for a rise of 60,000, just above November's downwardly revised increase of 56,000. The unemployment rate eased, as expected, to 4.4 per cent. Fed funds futures traders are now pricing in only a 4.8 per cent chance of a ‚Äårate cut at the Fed's January 27-28 meeting, down from 11.6 per cent before the data. ‚ÄåThe next cut appears unlikely before at least April. "Payrolls were a little bit light relative to consensus, but still fairly strong numbers," said Tim Ghriskey, senior portfolio strategist at Ingalls & Snyder in New York. "We are back to normal in terms of economic reporting so that's a bit of a relief for everyone." The release of U.S. economic data had been delayed because of the long federal government shutdown. The S&P 500 hit a record intraday high. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 213.52 points, or 0.43 per cent, to 49,480.41, the S&P 500 rose ‚Äç35.68 points, or 0.52 per cent, to 6,957.36 and the Nasdaq Composite rose 161.99 points, or 0.69 per cent, to 23,640.92. All three indexes are set for weekly gains in the first full trading week of 2026, with the Dow on track for its biggest weekly gain since the last week of November. MSCI's gauge of stocks across the globe gained 4.55 points, or 0.44 per cent, to 1,034.00.The pan-European STOXX 600 index rose 0.96 per cent. DOLLAR UP, ‚ÄãYIELDS DOWN After the jobs report the dollar initially gave ‚Äåup almost all the day's gains versus a basket of major currencies, having risen by nearly 0.2 per cent earlier. The dollar index, which measures the greenback against a basket of currencies including the yen and the euro, was ‚Äãlast up 0.28 per cent to 99.16.¬Ý U.S. Treasury yields were down slightly after initially rising on the data. The yield on benchmark U.S. 10-year notes fell ‚ÅÝ1.2 basis points to 4.171 per cent, from 4.183 per cent late on ‚ÄåThursday. SUPREME COURT WON'T RULE ON TRUMP'S TARIFFS FRIDAY Investors had been bracing for a possible U.S. Supreme Court ruling on the legality ‚Äãof President Donald Trump's tariffs.¬Ý But the court is expected to issue its next rulings on January 14. The court indicated on its website on Friday that it could release decisions in argued cases when the justices take ‚Äçthe bench during a scheduled sitting next Wednesday. In commodities, crude oil was sharply higher. U.S. crude rose 2.87 per cent to $59.42 a barrel and ‚ÅÝBrent rose to $63.53 per barrel, up 2.48 per cent on the day. Investors have become more convinced that production in Venezuela, even under U.S. control, may not rise meaningfully ‚Äãfor some time.¬Ý

data.title: Schrodinger to offer Eli Lilly's AI drug discovery platform on its software
data.description: Jan 9 : Schrodinger said on Friday it is collaborating with Eli Lilly to offer the pharmaceutical major's AI-based platform, TuneLab, on its drug designing software. Integration of Lilly's TuneLab into Schrodinger's LiveDesign will give biotech companies direct access to the artificial intelligence platform to help speed up ‚Äådrug development, the biotech software maker said. Schrodinger's cloud-based ‚Äåplatform, LiveDesign, helps chemists design compounds and predict the properties such as absorption and distribution of the experimental drug, helping them understand how a drug will behave in the body. Drug developers are increasing adoption of AI for discovery and safety testing ‚Äçto get faster and cheaper results, in line with an FDA push to reduce animal testing in the near future. Current LiveDesign clients will be able to use TuneLab in the first quarter of ‚Äãthis year, while the AI ‚Äåsoftware will be available to new users by the second quarter, Schrodinger's Chief Strategy Officer Karen Akinsanya said. Lilly ‚Äãlaunched TuneLab, an AI and machine learning platform, last year to ‚ÅÝprovide biotech companies access to ‚Äådrug discovery models trained on years of its research data. ‚ÄãThe drugmaker has already announced partnerships with several biotech companies to use its platform to develop drugs. "More biotechs ‚Äçusing the models means more diverse training data... Ultimately, this is ‚ÅÝabout moving molecules through discovery faster for the patients who are ‚Äãwaiting,"¬ÝAliza Apple, global head ‚Äåof Lilly TuneLab, said.

data.title: Exclusive-Philippines cenbank sees inflation rising toward target, rate cut cycle near end
data.description: Jan 9 : The ‚ÄåPhilippines' central bank will keep weighing a feeble growth outlook against gradually rising inflation to set monetary policy in a balancing act that could bring an end to its easing cycle soon, Deputy Governor Zeno Abenoja said on Friday. "Inflation has the greatest weight in the policy discussions at the Monetary Board," he told the Reuters Global Markets Forum. "For the next two years, inflation will gradually go up, approaching the midpoint of the target, and that's one reason why we think ... monetary policy easing could be nearing its end," Abenoja said, noting that near- and medium-term inflation are manageable. INFLATION ACCELERATED IN DECEMBER In ‚ÄåDecember, annual inflation rose at¬Ýits fastest pace in nine months, while monthly inflation registered the ‚Äåsharpest increase since September 2023. Still, average inflation for 2025 stood at 1.7 per cent, the slowest since 2016.¬Ý Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) targets inflation in the range of 2 per cent-4 per cent over the medium term. Economic momentum, meanwhile, weakened sharply in the third quarter, when growth slowed to 4.0 per cent year-on-year, missing an estimate of 5.2 per cent and down from 5.5 per cent in the second quarter, as a corruption scandal linked to government infrastructure projects dented consumer and investor confidence. Fourth-quarter GDP data is scheduled for release on January 29. "What is key right now is the uncertainty in the economic momentum ‚Äçthat we are seeing," Abenoja said, as third-quarter weakness might spill over into the first few months of 2026.¬ÝHe was, however, hopeful for a better growth number for the second-half of 2026, and even greater momentum in 2027. Growth in 2025 is forecast to ease to 4.6 per cent, below the government‚Äôs 5.5 per cent-6.5 per cent target, compared with 5.7 per cent last year. BSP projects 2026 growth in the 5 per cent-6 per cent range, and at 5.5 per cent-6.5 per cent for 2027. NEXT MONETARY POLICY SESSION ‚ÄãON FEBRUARY 19 The central bank cut its policy ‚Äårate for five straight meetings last year, bringing its benchmark rate to a three-year low of 4.5 per cent. Its next monetary policy meeting is scheduled for February 19. The government's 2026 budget provides for spending to be kept under control to ensure ‚Äãtaxpayer money is used responsibly, even as an earlier BSP forecast sees the country's 2025 current account deficit at $15.5 billion, or 3.2 per cent of GDP, ‚ÅÝslightly narrower than October's forecast. "The current account is mainly a ‚Äåreflection of still improving investment ratios, to fill in the infrastructure gap, and to increase productivity (and) potential output moving forward," Abenoja said, adding ‚Äãthat the Philippines needs more investments and external financing. "Fiscal consolidation continues, but probably not as steep as or as fast as previously envisioned, given all the external developments," he added. Abenoja also reiterated the BSP's position that it will ‚Äçnot defend the currency as the economics don't warrant it. The peso has been hovering around its all-time low of 59.362 per dollar, hit in ‚ÅÝDecember, underperforming many of its emerging Asian peers in 2025 amid dollar weakness. "We have to recognize certain periods where you can have big demand for dollars ... It's ‚Äãa healthy environment," he said. "But we're ‚Äålooking for those episodes that would have asymmetric effects on inflation." (Join GMF on LSEG Messenger for live interviews: ) (Reporting ‚Äçby ‚ÄãAnkika Biswas in Bengaluru, Editing by Divya Chowdhury and Mark Heinrich)

data.title: EU considers making WhatsApp more responsible for tackling harmful content, spokesperson says
data.description: BRUSSELS, ‚ÄåJan 9 : The European Commission is considering recategorizing Meta's WhatsApp messaging service to make it more responsible ‚Äåfor tackling illegal and ‚Äåharmful content, a spokesperson for the commission said on Friday. The spokesperson said the commission is ‚Äçactively considering designating WhatsApp a "very large platform", which would increase its legal responsibilities ‚Äãto ‚Äåaddress damaging content, after it published user ‚Äãnumbers above the Digital Services ‚ÅÝAct threshold in ‚ÄåFebruary 2025. "I wouldn't ‚Äãexclude a future designation," spokesperson Thomas Regnier ‚Äçtold reporters.

data.title: Democratic senators demand Apple, Google take X and Grok off app stores over sexual images
data.description: WASHINGTON, ‚ÄåJan 9 : Three Democratic U.S. senators are calling on Apple and Alphabet's Google to remove X and its built-in chabot Grok from ‚Äåtheir respective app stores ‚Äåover the spread of nonconsensual sexual images of women and minors on the platform. In a letter published Friday, ‚Äçsenators Ron Wyden of Oregon, Ben Ray Lujan of New Mexico, and Edward Markey ‚Äãof Massachusetts ‚Äåsaid Google and Apple "must remove these apps from ‚Äãthe app stores until X‚Äôs ‚ÅÝpolicy violations are ‚Äåaddressed." Google and Apple didn't ‚Äãimmediately return messages seeking comment. X did not ‚Äçimmediately return a message. The letter ‚ÅÝwas first reported by ‚ÄãNBC News.

data.title: Democratic senators demand Apple, Google take X and Grok off app stores over sexual images - NBC News
data.description: WASHINGTON, ‚ÄåJan 9 : Three Democratic U.S. senators are calling ‚Äåon Apple and ‚ÄåAlphabet's Google to remove X and Grok from ‚Äçtheir respective app stores over the spread ‚Äãof ‚Äånonconsensual sexual images ‚Äãof women and ‚ÅÝminors ‚Äåon the ‚Äãplatforms, NBC News reported ‚ÄçFriday.

data.title: Dollar gains against major currencies following US jobs data
data.description: NEW ‚ÄåYORK, Jan 9 : The dollar gained on Friday after data showed slower than expected U.S. jobs growth, suggesting¬Ýthe Federal Reserve could leave interest rates unchanged later this month. Financial markets had been bracing for a probable Supreme Court decision that could strike down President Donald Trump's tariffs. But the court will not issue that ruling on Friday. The U.S. economy added 50,000 jobs in December, according ‚Äåto Labor Department data on Friday. That was lower than ‚Äåan estimate of 60,000 jobs growth forecast by economists polled by Reuters. The dollar rose marginally across peer currencies as the data before paring those gains.¬Ý The greenback was up 0.72 per cent to 158 against the Japanese yen and was up 0.25 per cent to 0.801 against the Swiss franc. The euro was down 0.22 per cent against the dollar at $1.1633. The dollar index rose 0.27 per cent ‚Äçto 99.14. "In real life, the standard error margin for non-farm payrolls is 20,000 and so I don't think the market is going to pay much to this," said Steve Englander, head of global G10 FX Research at Standard Chartered. "If the Supreme Court rules on tariffs, I ‚Äãthink that will be the most ‚Äåimportant development of the day." Trump invoked the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA)¬Ýto impose tariffs without the approval of Congress. If the decision goes against Trump, company ‚Äãexecutives, customs brokers and trade lawyers are girding for a possible fight to secure refunds of ‚ÅÝabout¬Ý$150 billion¬Ýfrom the U.S. "It's possible the ‚ÄåCourt could find a way to mitigate the impact of the fiscal stream in ‚Äãterms of refunds or what they can do in the future. But¬Ýif they completely strike everything down, we actually think the bond market will ‚Äçreact really badly," Englander added. Fed funds futures are pricing an implied probability of 95 per cent that the ‚ÅÝcentral bank holds interest rates at its next two-day meet on January 27 and 28, up ‚Äãfrom 68 per cent a month ago, ‚Äåthe CME Group's FedWatch tool shows.¬Ý

data.title: TSMC fourth-quarter revenue jumps 20%, beats forecasts
data.description: TAIPEI, Jan ‚Äå9 : TSMC, the world's largest contract chipmaker, on Friday reported a 20.45 per cent increase in fourth-quarter revenue from a year earlier, beating the market forecast, as demand for the company's products leapt in response to surging interest in AI applications. The company, whose customers include Nvidia ‚Äåand Apple, has been a major beneficiary of ‚Äåadvances in AI, which has more than offset the tapering off of pandemic-led demand for chips used in consumer electronics like tablets. Revenue for the October-December period was T$1.046 trillion ($33.11 billion), according to Reuters calculations based on monthly data released by ‚Äçthe company, compared with T$868.46¬Ýbillion in the year-ago period. The latest result beat a LSEG SmartEstimate of T$1.036 trillion ($32.79 billion)¬Ýdrawn from 20 analysts, and was in line with guidance of $32.2 billion to $33.4 ‚Äãbillion issued by TSMC ‚Äåin October in its last earnings call. TSMC only gives guidance in U.S. dollars. TSMC will report full fourth-quarter ‚Äãearnings on January 15, when it is expected to provide updated ‚ÅÝguidance for the current quarter ‚Äåand full year, including its capital expenditure plans and ‚Äãrevenue growth outlook. TSMC's Taipei-listed shares gained 44.2 per cent last year, outperforming the 25.7 per cent rise for the broader market. Taiwan's ‚ÄçFoxconn, the world's largest contract electronics maker and Nvidia's biggest server ‚ÅÝmaker, also reported bumper sales on Monday, logging T$2.6028 trillion ($82.20 billion) ‚Äãfor the fourth quarter. ($1 = ‚Äå31.5950 Taiwan dollars)

data.title: Meta strikes nuclear power agreements with three companies
data.description: WASHINGTON, Jan ‚Äå9 : Meta Platforms said on Friday it struck 20-year agreements to buy power from three Vistra nuclear plants in the U.S. heartland and develop projects with two companies hoping to build small modular reactors. Shares of Oklo surged nearly 20 per cent, whereas Vistra rose about 8 per cent in premarket trade. Meta and other Big Tech companies want to secure long-term electricity supplies as artificial intelligence and data centers increase U.S. power demand for the first time in two decades. The company said in ‚Äåa blog it will purchase power from Vistra's Perry and Davis-Besse plants ‚Äåin Ohio and Beaver Valley plant in Pennsylvania. Meta said the deal will help finance expansion at the Ohio plants and lengthen the lifespan of the plants, which are licensed to run through at least 2036 with one of two reactors at Beaver Valley licensed through 2047. Meta will also help develop small modular reactors planned by Oklo and TerraPower, the latter of which is backed by billionaire Bill Gates. SMR backers say ‚Äçthe reactors will one day save costs because they can be built in factories instead of on site. Critics say they will struggle to achieve economies of scale similar to current large reactors. There are no U.S. SMRs in commercial operations yet and the plants will require permits. Joel Kaplan, Meta's chief global affairs officer, ‚Äãsaid the plans along with its agreement ‚Äålast year with Constellation to keep an Illinois reactor operating for 20 years will "make Meta one of the most significant corporate purchasers of nuclear energy in American history." The agreements will ‚Äãprovide up to 6.6 gigawatts of nuclear power by 2035, Meta said. The size of a typical nuclear ‚ÅÝpower plant is about 1 GW. In 2024 ‚ÄåMeta sought interest from nuclear power developers for 1 to 4 gigawatts of nuclear power. Meta will help ‚Äãfund TerraPower's development of two reactors to generate up to 690 megawatts of power as early as 2032. The agreement also provides Meta with rights for energy from up ‚Äçto six other TerraPower reactors by 2035. TerraPower President and CEO Chris Levesque said the agreement will support ‚ÅÝrapid deployment of reactors. Meta said its partnership with Oklo will help develop up to 1.2 GW of energy in Ohio ‚Äãas early as 2030. The ‚Äåsupport will help "early procurement and development", said Jacob DeWitte, Oklo‚Äôs co-founder and CEO.

data.title: Dollar gains following US employment data
data.description: NEW YORK, : The dollar gained on Friday after data showed slower than expected U.S. jobs growth and as financial markets braced for a ‚Äåprobable Supreme Court decision ‚Äåthat could strike down President Donald Trump's tariffs.¬Ý¬Ý¬Ý¬Ý¬Ý¬Ý The U.S. economy added 50,000 jobs in December, according to Labor Department data on ‚ÄçFriday. That was lower than an estimate of 60,000 jobs growth forecast by economists polled by Reuters. The ‚Äãdollar rose ‚Äåacross peer currencies as the data suggests the Federal Reserve ‚Äãcould leave interest rates unchanged later ‚ÅÝthis month. The greenback¬Ýwas ‚Äåup¬Ý0.38 per cent¬Ýto¬Ý157.42 against the Japanese ‚Äãyen and was up 0.09 per cent against the Swiss franc. The euro ‚Äçwas down 0.14 per cent against the dollar ‚ÅÝat $1.1644. The dollar index rose¬Ý0.13 per cent¬Ýto¬Ý99.01. (Reporting ‚Äãby Chibuike Oguh ‚Äåin New York; ‚ÄçEditing ‚Äãby Chizu Nomiyama )

data.title: Global stocks rise, dollar dips after US jobs miss expectations
data.description: LONDON, Jan 9 : ‚ÄåGlobal shares rallied and the dollar pared gains on Friday after data showed the U.S. economy created fewer jobs than expected in December, which could reinforce the case for more Federal Reserve rate cuts this year. The Bureau of Labor Statistics monthly report showed 50,000 workers were added to nonfarm payrolls in December, compared with expectations in a Reuters poll for a rise of 60,000, just above November's downwardly revised increase of 56,000. The unemployment rate eased, as expected, to 4.4 per cent. U.S. stock futures rose 0.3-0.4 per cent, building on earlier gains. The S&P 500 .SPX ended flat on Thursday, although an aerospace and ‚Äådefence index rose to an all-time high, with European defence shares also hitting a new ‚Äåhigh. Europe's STOXX 600 was last up 0.9 per cent, from a gain of 0.64 per cent earlier. "Friday's weaker-than-expected jobs report showed that hiring continued to slow towards the end of 2025, capping a tough year for the labour market," Dennis Follmer, chief investment officer at Montis Financial, in Massachusetts, which manages $1.3 billion, said. "With a growing economy and the Fed still in rate-cutting mode, the AI boom likely still has some legs for 2026, but there are increasing signs that it is getting late at what has been a great party and some guests are getting a ‚Äçbit carried away," he said. DOLLAR RETREATS SLIGHTLY The dollar gave up almost all the day's gains versus a basket of major currencies, having risen by nearly 0.2 per cent earlier. The euro was flat at $1.1647 from $1.1641 earlier, while the Japanese yen strengthened a touch to 157.52, leaving the dollar up 0.42 per cent on the day, compared with 157.595 before the jobs report. Markets show traders still expect two rate cuts by the end of this year, ‚Äãmeaning the payrolls report did not change that ‚Äåview. Gold, which tends to perform well in an environment of falling U.S. rates, given that it bears no yield, was up 0.2 per cent on the day at $4,485 an ounce, compared with a 0.14 per cent daily loss earlier. U.S. two-year Treasury yields were ‚Äãup 2.5 basis points at 3.513 per cent, unchanged from levels before the employment numbers, while yields on the benchmark 10-year Treasury note dipped to 4.179 per cent, steady ‚ÅÝon the day, from around 4.191 per cent earlier on. SUPREME COURT ‚ÄåRULING IMMINENT Later on Friday, the U.S. Supreme Court may rule on the legality of President Donald Trump's tariffs. Some investors believe stocks could ‚Äãbounce if the court rolls back existing tariffs, especially for companies that had to absorb high-import costs. "A constraint may be that even if the tariffs are ruled unlawful, the Trump administration is unlikely to roll over and will ‚Äçlook to other ways to maintain the levies," Kyle Rodda, senior financial markets analyst at Capital.com, said.¬Ý In commodities, crude oil held on to ‚ÅÝits gains. Brent futures were up 0.6 per cent on the day at $62.36 a barrel, set for a roughly 3 per cent gain this week, as investors have become more convinced ‚Äãthat production in Venezuela, even under U.S. ‚Äåcontrol, may not rise meaningfully for some time.¬Ý

data.title: TSMC posts Q4 revenue of T$1,046.08 billion, above forecasts
data.description: (Corrects ‚Äåto show revenue amount is in Taiwan dollars, not U.S. dollars, in headline) TAIPEI, Jan 9 : TSMC, the world's largest ‚Äåcontract chipmaker, reported ‚Äåon Friday fourth-quarter revenue of T$1,046.08 billion ($33.05 billion), according to Reuters calculations based on monthly data the ‚Äçcompany released. The fourth-quarter revenue beat market forecasts, and was up 20.45 per cent from the ‚Äãyear ago ‚Äåperiod on surging interest in artificial intelligence ‚Äãapplications. An LSEG SmartEstimate, drawn from 20¬Ýanalysts, ‚ÅÝpredicted fourth-quarter ‚Äårevenue of T$1,035.913 ‚Äãbillion ($32.73 billion).¬Ý Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) is a major ‚Äçsupplier to companies including Nvidia ‚ÅÝand Apple. ($1 = 31.6550 Taiwan ‚Äãdollars)

data.title: DeepSeek to launch new AI model focused on coding in February, The Information reports
data.description: Jan ‚Äå9 : Chinese AI startup DeepSeek is expected to ‚Äålaunch ‚Äåits next-generation AI model V4 that features ‚Äçstrong coding capabilities in February, The ‚ÄãInformation ‚Äåreported on Friday, ‚Äãciting people familiar ‚ÅÝwith ‚Äåthe ‚Äãmatter. Reuters could not immediately ‚Äçverify the ‚ÅÝreport.

data.title: Germany plans measures to combat harmful AI image manipulation
data.description: BERLIN, Jan 9 : ‚ÄåGermany's justice ministry plans to present measures in the near future that would allow authorities to more effectively combat the use of artificial intelligence to manipulate images in ways that violate personal rights, a spokesperson said on Friday. Grok, the built-in AI chatbot on billionaire Elon Musk's social media site X, has come under investigation in Europe for its so-called "spicy mode", ‚Äåwhich allows users to generate sexually explicit images. A Reuters ‚Äåinvestigation found the chatbot's image generation was being used to create images of women and children in minimal clothing, often without the consent of the individuals depicted. Germany's media minister urged the European Commission earlier this week to take legal action to stop what he called the "industrialisation of sexual harassment" on X. Responding to a question ‚Äçabout the controversy at a regularly held government press conference, justice ministry spokesperson Anna-Lena Beckfeld indicated that Germany was preparing to take on the issue in its domestic courts. "It is unacceptable that manipulation on a large scale is being used for systematic violations ‚Äãof personal rights," she said. "We, ‚Äåtherefore, want to ensure that criminal law can be used more effectively to combat this." The ministry is working to better regulate deepfakes and ‚Äãplans a law against digital violence to support its victims, she told journalists. "We want to ‚ÅÝmake it easier for them to ‚Äåtake direct action against violations of their rights on the internet," Beckfeld said. The ‚Äãministry plans to present concrete proposals in the near future, she said, adding that she could not comment in detail on the plans ‚Äçat this point. After initially dismissing concerns over Grok's image generation, xAI has now restricted ‚ÅÝthe function to paid subscribers. Musk said last week that anyone using the chatbot to create ‚Äãillegal content would face ‚Äåthe same consequences as uploading such material directly.
